adrs.title: Use Better Auth (not NextAuth) for OAuth + session management
adrs.context: App needs OIDC auth against Ministry Platform with a non-standard provider (custom realm param, MP-specific scope, custom userinfo endpoint). Must work with Next.js 16 App Router and Turbopack.
adrs.decision: Better Auth 1.5 with genericOAuth plugin. Evidence of NextAuth history: env var fallbacks (NEXTAUTH_URL, NEXTAUTH_SECRET) preserved for gradual migration.
adrs.consequences: Custom getUserInfo and mapProfileToUser needed for MP OIDC quirks. TypeScript type inference gaps for additionalFields require casts. First-class Next.js 16 integration via nextCookies() plugin + toNextJsHandler(auth).
adrs.alternatives: NextAuth v5 — heavier, less flexible with custom OIDC; Clerk/Auth0 — external dependencies not viable for on-prem MP; hand-rolled OAuth — maintenance burden.

adrs.title: Stateless JWT cookie sessions (no database adapter)
adrs.context: App is tools suite for internal MP users. Avoid extra infra (Postgres/Redis). Want fast getSession() without DB roundtrips.
adrs.decision: Enable session.cookieCache with strategy: 'jwt' and 1-hour TTL. Enable account.storeAccountCookie: true and storeStateStrategy: 'cookie'. No adapter configured.
adrs.consequences: No server-side session list or revocation. Server restarts don't invalidate sessions. But no cross-device logout, no admin session kill-switch. Refresh-token rotation in stateless mode unverified.
adrs.alternatives: DB adapter (Postgres/SQLite via Drizzle) — requires operating auth DB; Redis adapter — another dependency.

adrs.title: customSession does name-splitting only, profile loading moves to client
adrs.context: Temptation: enrich session with full MP user profile (roles, permissions, Image_GUID) inside customSession so all consumers get it for free.
adrs.decision: customSession does only firstName/lastName splitting from user.name. MP profile loading deferred to UserProvider on client, calling getCurrentUserProfile(userGuid) via server action.
adrs.consequences: getSession() stays fast (no MP API call on every request). Client components must mount UserProvider and read useUser(). One extra round-trip on page load.
adrs.alternatives: Profile inside customSession — would hit MP API on every cache miss and make sign-in brittle (MP down = sign-in broken).

adrs.title: Singleton MinistryPlatformProvider with shared client
adrs.context: Provider stack needs one authoritative token cache across all services; otherwise each service maintains own tokens, causing Nx OAuth traffic and 5-minute windows drifting out of sync.
adrs.decision: MinistryPlatformProvider is classical private-constructor + getInstance() singleton. Owns exactly one MinistryPlatformClient and passes to all six services at construction time.
adrs.consequences: Token cache is process-global and shared across all MPHelper instances. Testing requires mocking MinistryPlatformProvider.getInstance. No per-tenant isolation in-process.
adrs.alternatives: Per-request client — simpler testing but more OAuth churn; DI-based factory — flexible but boilerplate; module-level state — harder to reset in tests.

adrs.title: Layered facade / provider / services / client
adrs.context: MP API surface covers tables, procedures, files, communications, metadata, domain — each with own URL shape. Flat API would expose too many primitives and couple validation/param mapping to HTTP mechanics.
adrs.decision: Four layers: MPHelper (facade — friendly params + Zod), MinistryPlatformProvider (singleton orchestrator — delegates), six *Service classes (domain-specific HTTP), MinistryPlatformClient + HttpClient (auth + fetch).
adrs.consequences: Clear single-responsibility per layer. Swapping transport straightforward at client or service layer. Small operations require ~3 hops. New endpoint requires plumbing through three files.
adrs.alternatives: Flat MPHelper — loses singleton benefit + no per-domain modularity; DI services — Next.js server actions don't want setup overhead; direct fetch in services — loses token caching.

adrs.title: 5-minute hard-coded token life instead of using expires_in
adrs.context: MP OAuth returns expires_in: 3600 (1 hour), but production has observed tokens invalidated earlier (server restarts, credential rotations). Relying on expires_in causes 401 storms.
adrs.decision: Ignore expires_in. Hard-code TOKEN_LIFE = 5 * 60 * 1000. Refresh every 5 minutes regardless.
adrs.consequences: Predictable refresh cadence; at most 5 minutes of stale-token risk. Higher OAuth volume (~12x/hour vs once/hour). No mitigation for sub-5-minute revocations. No retry on 401.
adrs.alternatives: Use expires_in minus 60s buffer — fragile; retry-on-401 with single refresh — complicates error paths; heartbeat — extra cost.

adrs.title: Two independent token pipelines (default + dev)
adrs.context: api_dev_* stored procedures must run against non-production MP credentials to avoid accidental prod mutation during dev tooling (e.g., api_dev_DeployTool).
adrs.decision: MinistryPlatformClient holds two isolated (token, expiresAt, HttpClient) triples. CredentialProfile ('default' | 'dev') parameter selects env-var pair. ProcedureService routes only api_dev_* through dev pipeline.
adrs.consequences: Clean isolation — dev token refresh never touches prod token. Two HTTP clients in memory (minimal cost). Dev env vars optional; dev pipeline throws clear message if missing.
adrs.alternatives: Single pipeline with pre-call hook swapping credentials — error-prone; separate Client instances — equivalent but more wiring.

adrs.title: Separate services per MP REST concern (table, procedure, file, communication, metadata, domain)
adrs.context: MP REST API spans CRUD, stored procs, file uploads, communications, schema metadata, domain info. Monolithic client mixes concerns and test surfaces.
adrs.decision: Split into 6 single-responsibility service classes each taking shared MinistryPlatformClient via constructor. MinistryPlatformProvider (singleton) orchestrates; MPHelper adds app-level ergonomics.
adrs.consequences: Clean per-endpoint test files. Each service independently mockable. Callers go through MPHelper → Provider.
adrs.alternatives: Single MinistryPlatformClient with all methods; facade-only without class split.

adrs.title: Zod validation lives in MPHelper, not TableService
adrs.context: Validation is cross-cutting, applies only when callers opt in. Pushing into TableService forces every call site to think about it, and couples HTTP to Zod.
adrs.decision: TableService is pure HTTP. MPHelper.createTableRecords accepts optional `schema?: ZodObject` param, validates each record via schema.parse(), delegates to provider → TableService.
adrs.consequences: Opt-in validation: callers can skip for backward-compat. Partial vs full update (`partial: boolean`, default true) expressed via schema.partial() at helper layer. TableService stays thin.
adrs.alternatives: Validate in TableService directly; validate at server actions only.

adrs.title: Auto-generated MP schema docs regenerated via npm scripts
adrs.context: MP database has 301 tables and 532 stored procedures. Hand-maintaining a catalog would drift constantly.
adrs.decision: tables.md and stored-procs.md are output from `npm run mp:generate:models` and `npm run mp:generate:storedprocs`. required-procs.md is hand-written (app invokes only 7 of 532).
adrs.consequences: Schema docs stay in sync after regen; required-procs.md needs manual updates when new service methods wrap new procs.
adrs.alternatives: Hand-maintained catalog (untenable at scale); on-the-fly MP API lookup at agent time (latency, no offline grep).

adrs.title: Services use async singleton (getInstance) rather than factory
adrs.context: All 5 services need a single MPHelper across request lifecycle; construction is cheap but initialize may become async; tests need state reset.
adrs.decision: Each service: class with private constructor, private static instance, public static async getInstance(). Tests reset via `(Service as any).instance = undefined` in beforeEach.
adrs.consequences: Single shared instance per process; test isolation requires explicit reset; must await getInstance even though current initialize is sync.
adrs.alternatives: Module-level const service = new Service() — loses test resettability; factory returning new instances — wastes MPHelper construction.

adrs.title: Server actions call services (not MPHelper directly)
adrs.context: Components need typed MP operations but shouldn't know about MP URL/filter/select conventions; audit ($userId), validation, batching, error handling should live in one place.
adrs.decision: Actions go auth.api.getSession → Service.getInstance() → service.method() → MPHelper. Services own query strings, batching, validation, audit wiring.
adrs.consequences: Clean test seam: mock MPHelper once per service; query conventions change in one place.
adrs.alternatives: Actions call MPHelper directly — duplicates strings across features, drifts on ambiguous-column prefixes.

adrs.title: Separate session context from user context
adrs.context: Better Auth session contains only identity claims (id, email, userGuid). Downstream features need roles, groups, Contact_ID, image — all require MP API calls.
adrs.decision: Keep useAppSession minimal. Put enriched MP data behind dedicated UserProvider that loads lazily from userGuid.
adrs.consequences: Auth-only consumers avoid MP API latency; MP profile load happens once per session on client; refresh is explicit.
adrs.alternatives: Merge into single provider (couples auth to MP availability); load profile server-side in layout (blocks TTFB).

adrs.title: Dual-layer route protection: edge proxy + server-component session validation
adrs.context: Need fast rejection of unauthenticated requests at edge without paying for DB/JWT validation, while enforcing full validation before any MP API call. Better Auth's full getSession is Node-only and too heavy for edge.
adrs.decision: Layer 1 (src/proxy.ts) runs on edge, does getSessionCookie() presence check only. Layer 2 (src/components/layout/auth-wrapper.tsx) runs as server component inside (web) route group, calls auth.api.getSession() for full validation.
adrs.consequences: Fast happy path at edge; stale/forged cookies rejected at layer 2; userGuid available to downstream server actions. Two places to maintain auth logic.
adrs.alternatives: Single full-validation gate in proxy (edge can't cheaply run Better Auth internals); skip edge check entirely (every request hits server component render before redirecting).

adrs.title: Route group (web) for authenticated pages; /signin and /api/auth as siblings
adrs.context: Need all authenticated pages to share AuthWrapper + Providers without duplicating layout. /signin itself and Better Auth catch-all must render without a session.
adrs.decision: Use Next.js App Router route group src/app/(web)/ to apply shared layout. Public routes (/signin, /api/auth/[...all]) sit outside the group.
adrs.consequences: Clean separation. Adding a new tool is one-directory operation. Easy to accidentally place public page under (web)/.
adrs.alternatives: Per-page auth checks (copy-paste prone); single top-level layout with conditional branch (awkward in App Router).

adrs.title: Vitest (not Jest) as test runner
adrs.context: Need TS-first runner integrating with Turbopack/Vite-style config, supporting import.meta, running fast against ESM without transform.
adrs.decision: Vitest 4.x with @vitejs/plugin-react for JSX. Chosen over Jest to avoid Babel/SWC transform config, reuse vite resolver for @/ alias.
adrs.consequences: All mock patterns assume vi.mock() hoisting. No Jest-specific matchers; only @testing-library/jest-dom matchers. globals: true enables bare describe/it/expect.
adrs.alternatives: Jest with ts-jest or @swc/jest — transform overhead and duplicate resolver config.

adrs.title: jsdom (not happy-dom) as DOM environment
adrs.context: React component tests need DOM supporting localStorage, window.location, Headers, @testing-library APIs.
adrs.decision: environment: 'jsdom' with jsdom@^28.1.0. Widely supported by @testing-library.
adrs.consequences: Slightly slower than happy-dom on pure-DOM tests, but compatibility is higher for edge APIs (Headers, Storage).
adrs.alternatives: happy-dom — faster but historically has gaps around Headers/fetch/storage mocks.

adrs.title: Hand-written DTOs alongside generated MP models
adrs.context: src/lib/providers/ministry-platform/models/ contains 603 auto-generated types mirroring DB columns. UI/action layers need shapes that diverge (camelCase, composed fields).
adrs.decision: Keep generated MP models as DB-shape layer. Author application DTOs by hand in src/lib/dto/ (cross-feature) or co-located with features.
adrs.consequences: Pro: UI code stays camelCase; regenerating MP models won't break components. Con: Mapping code must exist.
adrs.alternatives: Use MP generated types directly (couples UI to snake_case); generate DTOs from MP schemas (adds tooling).

adrs.title: SQL-safety through throwing validators, not types
adrs.context: Service layer interpolates user input into MP OData $filter strings. Need prevention without a query builder.
adrs.decision: Four throwing helpers in src/lib/validation.ts (validateGuid, validatePositiveInt, validateColumnName, escapeFilterString). Callers interpolate return value directly.
adrs.consequences: Simple, testable. Static types cannot enforce 'this string was validated'.
adrs.alternatives: Branded/opaque types (over-engineered); full ORM (MP API doesn't benefit).

adrs.title: Hand-rolled USPS-B-3200 IMb encoder (no third-party library)
adrs.context: Need to print USPS IMb barcodes onto PDF labels and embed in Word. JS IMb libraries uncommon, often GPL, sometimes abandonware.
adrs.decision: Implement IMb end-to-end in src/lib/imb-encoder.ts: precomputed 5-of-13/2-of-13 tables, CRC-11 with poly 0x0F35, mixed-radix codeword decomposition, BAR_TABLE per USPS-B-3200. Verified against spec's example 4.
adrs.consequences: Full control; no licensing friction; ~400 lines with unit coverage. Team owns non-trivial spec implementation.
adrs.alternatives: Adopt JS IMb library (licensing + maintenance); server-side CLI (deployment complexity); third-party service (latency + dependency).

adrs.title: Use shadcn/ui over MUI/other component libraries
adrs.context: Tool pages need customizable primitives rendering with Tailwind v4 tokens and integrating with React Server Components.
adrs.decision: Install shadcn/ui primitives via CLI into src/components/ui/ using new-york style, slate base color, CSS variables, lucide icons.
adrs.consequences: Primitives in our repo, editable locally; no framework upgrade cycle; Tailwind utilities flow through. 22 files to maintain.
adrs.alternatives: MUI/Chakra (runtime CSS conflicts with Tailwind v4); Radix UI raw (no default styling).

adrs.title: Named exports only across src/components/
adrs.context: Mixed default/named exports make refactors noisy and barrel exports inconsistent.
adrs.decision: Every component file uses named exports; feature folders expose index.ts barrels; default exports forbidden.
adrs.consequences: Predictable import pattern, grep-friendly rename refactors. shadcn CLI sometimes emits default exports that must be converted.
adrs.alternatives: Default exports for top-level page components.

adrs.title: Pre-encode barcodes server-side into LabelData.barStates
adrs.context: IMb and POSTNET encoding require check-digit/CRC math. Running in PDF renderers couples @react-pdf/renderer to encoder state and duplicates work with Word path.
adrs.decision: Encode once in preEncodeBarcodes inside server action; store result as LabelData.barStates + LabelData.barType.
adrs.consequences: Barcode logic isolated and unit-testable. Both renderers accept same string format.
adrs.alternatives: Encode inside each renderer (duplicated); encode client-side (Mailer ID handling, no Node Buffer).

adrs.title: Client-only GrapesJS via next/dynamic(ssr:false)
adrs.context: GrapesJS and @grapesjs/react are DOM-bound and cannot run under Node SSR.
adrs.decision: Wrap EditorCanvas in next/dynamic with ssr:false and Skeleton fallback; mark every editor file 'use client'.
adrs.consequences: No SSR for editor; initial paint shows skeleton then hydrates. Rest of tools route stays server-rendered.
adrs.alternatives: Conditional import inside useEffect (harder to type).

adrs.title: Unify DevPanel and Deploy Tool into single collapsible overlay
adrs.context: Previously dev params overlay and separate deploy-tool affordance existed. They shared gating logic (localhost-only) and deploy tool was only useful when current tool not yet registered.
adrs.decision: Merge into one DevPanel shell composing sub-panels. DeployToolPanel renders conditionally based on UserToolsPanel authorization check. Successful deploy bumps refreshKey to re-run authorization.
adrs.consequences: Single collapsible bar reduces clutter. State coupling centralized in DevPanel. Adds dependency on NEXT_PUBLIC_PROD_URL.
adrs.alternatives: Keep two overlays separate (duplicated gating logic).

adrs.title: User-supplied Mailer ID persisted in localStorage (no env var)
adrs.context: USPS Mailer IDs issued per mailer, not per deployment; different users may use different IDs. Churches may not have one at all.
adrs.decision: LabelConfig.mailerId is form field defaulting to ''; persisted client-side in localStorage key 'address-labels-config'. preEncodeBarcodes falls back to POSTNET when missing.
adrs.consequences: No server-side env required; each user configures own. IMb generation silently disabled until valid ID entered.
adrs.alternatives: Env var (doesn't scale per-user); MP custom field (overkill).
